title: lupinus sierrae-blancae subsp. aquilinus
intro: Lupinus sierrae-blancae subsp. aquilinus, a rare and lesser-known subspecies of lupine, is a perennial plant native to the Sierra Blanca region in New Mexico, USA. This plant is rich in medicinal properties, particularly its alkaloid content, which includes sparteine and lupanine, known for their analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Lupinus sierrae-blancae subsp. aquilinus include its potential to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as its use as a diuretic to aid in urinary issues. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions.
